content:
🔥 whisperbot | C
🎯 Primary Use Case: Transcribing Telegram voice messages into text automatically.
✨ Key Features: • Transcribes audio messages from Telegram using whisper.cpp or qwen-asr • Handles audio downloading and conversion to WAV • Streams transcription results back to Telegram as they are generated • Manages ASR processing queue to balance latency and throughput • Dynamically switches between faster and better ASR models based on queue length
📖 Summary: Whisperbot is a Telegram bot written in C that transcribes audio messages into text using either whisper.cpp or qwen-asr as the backend. It handles audio conversion, transcription processing, and streams the text back to the user, with options for managing queue length and model selection for performance.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🚀 FireRedVAD | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: Accurate detection of voice activity and specific audio events in various languages for applications requiring audio analysis.
✨ Key Features: • State-of-the-art Voice Activity Detection (VAD) • Industrial-grade Audio Event Detection (AED) • Supports 100+ languages • Detects speech, singing, and music • Supports non-streaming and streaming VAD
📖 Summary: FireRedVAD is a state-of-the-art Voice Activity Detection (VAD) and Audio Event Detection (AED) solution. It supports over 100 languages for speech, singing, and music detection, offering both streaming and non-streaming capabilities. The system achieves high accuracy, outperforming existing VAD solutions on benchmark datasets.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

💡 cloudflare-solver | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: Automating the bypassing of Cloudflare's anti-bot challenges to obtain `cf_clearance` cookies or Turnstile tokens for programmatic access to Cloudflare-protected websites.
✨ Key Features: • Dual Challenge Support (Challenge and Turnstile) • Asynchronous operation using asyncio and Playwright • Stealthy browser fingerprinting with Camoufox and BrowserForge • Modular OOP design • Configurable options (retries, delays, OS)
📖 Summary: This Python repository provides an asynchronous solution for bypassing Cloudflare's anti-bot challenges, specifically Turnstile and Challenge types. It aims to automate the process of obtaining necessary cookies or tokens, supporting features like proxy usage and realistic browser fingerprinting for stealth.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🚀 netsnap | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: Quickly and easily switching between different network configurations on a Windows machine, such as moving between office, home, and on-site network environments, without manual GUI interaction.
✨ Key Features: • CLI utility for network profile switching on Windows • Store network configurations as named profiles in JSON • Apply profiles with a single command (e.g., `netsnap apply work`) • Supports static IP, DHCP, gateway, and DNS settings
📖 Summary: netsnap is a command-line utility for Windows that simplifies switching between pre-configured network profiles. It allows users to save and apply different network settings (IP address, gateway, DNS) with a single command, eliminating the need for manual configuration through the GUI.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

💡 retroassembly | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: Playing retro video games in a web browser with a curated collection interface.
✨ Key Features: • Play numerous retro gaming consoles (NES, SNES, Genesis, GameBoy, Arcade, etc.) in the browser. • Visually browse game collection with auto-detected box arts. • Save and synchronize game progress. • Rewind gameplay (where supported by emulator).
📖 Summary: RetroAssembly is a web-based personal retro game collection cabinet that allows users to play classic games directly in their browser. It features a visually appealing library display with auto-detected box arts, save/load functionality, rewind capabilities, and intuitive spatial navigation for browsing games.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source
